reasoning: CVS has been on a very strong uptrend throughout 2025, surging from around $45 in January to $81.26 by late October on the back of consecutive earnings beats and profit guidance upgrades. The strong positive momentum suggests a slightly better than 50/50 chance of continuing the positive streak in the very short term. However, weekly stock moves are highly unpredictable and subject to mean reversion despite overarching trends. Following specific guidance that distant dates must carry more uncertainty and probabilities should revert closer to 0.5 as the forecast horizon extends, I project a slight upward bias (0.54) for the nearest horizon (1 week), stepping down to 0.52 for the 1-month horizon, and 0.51 for the 3-month horizon, reflecting increasing uncertainty.
